title: task
description: secure.rs:239 - decrypt_response() calls ctx.unprotect_request() to decrypt OSCORE responses, but OSCORE responses have different AAD construction than requests per RFC 8613 Section 5.4. The function should call an unprotect_response() method (or equivalent). Failure scenario: (1) Legitimate OSCORE responses from peers will fail authentication because the AAD verification uses request-format AAD instead of response-format AAD, causing DecryptFailed errors. (2) If the underlying OSCORE implementation doesn't properly distinguish request/response AAD, this could be a security vulnerability allowing AAD confusion attacks.
close_reason: Already fixed: decrypt_response correctly calls unprotect_response at line 250, with SECURITY comment explaining RFC 8613 semantics
